figures.caption: FIG. 1. Schematic of the inertial conical-collapse problem. The gas cavity approaches a cone of semiangle β, described using the polar distance r and angle θ measured from the apparent apex, or equivalently the axial and radial coordinates z and rp. The jet radius rj and velocity vj define the local observables used to test the self-similar scaling.

FIG. 4. (a) Values of ν solving Eq. (4) as a function of the cone angle β; the grey symbols are the cone angles measured in the DNS. The red dot marks Taylor’s cone, β = 49.3◦, ν = 0.5. (b) Exponent α = 1/(2− ν) as a function of Oh for Bo = 0 (levels 13–15) and Bo = 10−3 (level 13), using the same cone fits; marker shapes distinguish refinement levels. The dashed line marks α = 2/3.

FIG. 2. DNS evidence for inertial focusing at Bo = 0 and Oh = 0.03. (a) Velocity magnitude, streamlines, and interface shapes near jet inception; the four snapshots (i)–(iv) correspond to τ − τ0 = −1.9 × 10−3, −2.1 × 10−4, +2.3 × 10−3, and +5.4× 10−3, respectively. Orange curves mark the interface, grey curves the streamlines, and colour gives |u|. (b) Jet-feeding flux Qj = 2π ∫ vzr dr = πrjqj . (c) Local Weber number Wej = Q2 j/(π 2r3j ) = q2j /rj . Symbols show DNS for MAXlevel=13– 15 and, at MAXlevel=15, focus levels 13–15 (defined in the End Matter). The shaded band marks the asymptotic window 0.005 ≤ rj ≤ 0.024 used to set the conical-inertial and inertio-capillary prefactors. Lines denote the present theory with α = 0.629, the inertio-capillary scaling with α = 2/3, and the finite-radius scaling of Ref. [27]. The large values and growth of Wej in the asymptotic window rule out a local inertio-capillary balance, which would require Wej = O(1).
